text: A box contains 5 identical balls. One ball is heavier than the rest. Using a balance scale only twice, how can you find the heaviest ball?
optionB: Compare three balls vs two balls

A city park is becoming less popular because visitors feel it is boring and unattractive. The city council has a limited budget but wants to attract more people to the park. Which idea shows the most creative solution?
optionA: Increase the entry fee to maintain the park
optionB: Add simple interactive activities like art walls and community events
optionC: Close the park earlier to reduce maintenance costs
correctAnswer: Add simple interactive activities like art walls and community events

text: A school wants students to develop creativity and teamwork during a science project. Which approach would best encourage creative thinking?
optionA: Give students exact instructions to follow step by step
optionB: Allow students to design their own experiments and present their ideas
optionC: Ask students to copy experiments from textbooks
optionD: Focus only on memorizing scientific formulas
correctAnswer: Allow students to design their own experiments and present their ideas

text: A small company wants to improve employee motivation without increasing salaries due to budget limits. Which strategy demonstrates the most creative thinking?
optionB: Provide recognition programs, flexible schedules, and opportunities for skill development
optionD: Reduce break times to increase productivity
correctAnswer: Provide recognition programs, flexible schedules, and opportunities for skill development

text: A list of numbers is described as advancing progressively in such a way that each successive element exceeds the immediately preceding element by a constant and unvarying quantity. The actual numerical values themselves remain undisclosed, and no information is provided concerning either the origin or the termination of the list. The only characteristic that has been explicitly specified is that the incremental difference between consecutive elements remains perfectly consistent throughout the entirety of the sequence. Based solely on this description, can the sequence be accurately classified as an arithmetic sequence?

A certain operational procedure is described as unfolding through a structured progression consisting of four clearly distinguishable stages. It is explicitly indicated that the commencement of the third stage cannot occur until the second stage has been entirely completed. Additionally, the fourth stage is defined as an event that occurs exclusively at the ultimate conclusion of the entire procedure. The first stage, interestingly, is characterized not by a specific activity of its own but rather by the absence of the conditions associated with the fourth stage. Taking all of these constraints into consideration, which stage must logically constitute the starting point of the process?

An individual explains that the commencement of his professional duties took place at a time precisely fifteen minutes after the completion of the sixth consecutive hour measured from the exact moment at which the sun rose above the horizon. For the sake of analytical clarity, assume that sunrise occurs uniformly at exactly 06:00 in the morning, regardless of geographical or seasonal variations. When the temporal description provided by the individual is translated into standard clock notation, which specific time accurately corresponds to the beginning of his work?

A person asserts that he possesses a rectangular container whose interior currently contains a certain quantity of liquid. He further specifies that the liquid occupies precisely one quarter of the container’s total internal capacity. However, the container itself bears no markings, measurements, or indicators that might reveal the level of liquid within it. If an observer is nevertheless required to describe the proportion of the container that is presently filled in the most exact fractional terms possible, which of the following fractions most accurately represents the quantity of liquid contained inside?

Three containers, identified respectively as A, B, and C, are initially described as holding identical volumes of liquid under normal conditions. It is later reported that the entire contents of container A have been transferred into container B. Subsequently, a certain portion of the liquid contained in container B is transferred into container C, resulting in container C holding an amount of liquid equal to exactly half of its total capacity. Assuming that no liquid is lost during these transfers and that the operations are performed with perfect accuracy, which container ultimately contains the greatest volume of liquid?
optionA: Container A holds the greatest volume
optionB: Container B holds the greatest volume
optionC: Container C holds the greatest volume
correctAnswer: Container B holds the greatest volume

text: Consider a situation in which every element belonging to a particular set is defined as possessing a specific property. It is further stated that a certain object unquestionably belongs to this set. Without introducing any additional assumptions or external information, what logical conclusion can be drawn regarding the relationship between the object and the property that characterizes the entire set?
optionA: The object must possess the property
optionB: The object cannot possess the property
optionC: The object may or may not possess the property
correctAnswer: The object must possess the property

text: A process is described as unfolding through a rigidly structured sequence in which each step can only occur after the immediate completion of the preceding step. No stage is permitted to overlap with another, and no stage may begin prematurely. If such a process is analyzed purely in terms of its operational logic, what fundamental organizational principle best describes the structure of this procedure?

A researcher describes a numerical pattern in which each new value is obtained by adding a constant quantity to the value that came immediately before it. This addition remains identical for every step throughout the pattern, and no deviations from this rule are ever introduced. From the standpoint of mathematical classification, what defining characteristic best describes this pattern?

A statement declares that a particular rule applies universally to every possible instance within a defined system and that no exceptions are permitted under any circumstances. If an individual instance within the system fails to conform to this rule, what logical conclusion must necessarily follow?

A description explains that a certain event recurs repeatedly at perfectly regular intervals of time. The duration separating each occurrence remains identical throughout the entire observation period, and no irregularities or disruptions are ever recorded. What general type of pattern does this situation most accurately represent?

A particular system of organization is described as one in which every subsequent action is entirely dependent upon the successful completion of the action that precedes it. No action may begin prematurely, nor may two actions occur simultaneously. Each stage must be executed in its proper order to ensure the integrity of the entire process. From the standpoint of structural analysis, which principle most accurately characterizes this form of organization?

An observer notices that a sequence of numbers evolves according to a rule whereby each number is produced by multiplying the preceding number by a fixed and unchanging quantity. The initial value of the sequence is not revealed, yet the multiplicative relationship remains perfectly consistent for every step that follows. Based solely on this information, how should the sequence be classified?

A regulation states that a specific activity may only occur if two independent conditions have both been satisfied beforehand. Should either condition remain unfulfilled, the activity in question is explicitly prohibited from taking place. If an observer determines that neither of the required conditions has been met, what conclusion logically follows?

A water reservoir is described as containing a quantity of liquid equal to precisely one half of its total capacity. If an identical quantity of water is subsequently added to the reservoir, resulting in the container becoming completely full, what fraction of the reservoir’s capacity was originally empty?

A description of a structured workflow indicates that the final stage of the process can only occur once all preceding stages have been fully completed. No shortcuts or omissions are permitted, and each stage must contribute to the completion of the next. From a logical perspective, what characteristic best describes the relationship between the stages?

A rule within a system specifies that every member belonging to a certain category must possess a particular attribute. If an individual object is confirmed to belong to that category, what logical inference can be made regarding that object?
optionA: The object must possess the attribute
optionB: The object cannot possess the attribute
correctAnswer: The object must possess the attribute

START
READ n
isPrime = True
FOR i = 2 TO n-1
    IF n % i == 0 THEN
        isPrime = False
        BREAK
    END IF
END FOR
IF isPrime AND n > 1 THEN
    PRINT "Prime"
ELSE
    PRINT "Not Prime"
END IF
END

If n = 13, what will the program print?

START
READ n
rev = 0
WHILE n > 0
    digit = n % 10
    rev = rev * 10 + digit
    n = n / 10
END WHILE
PRINT rev
END
